summary: Resolve three Fabric dogfood UX pain-points (fabric-import interrupted by Stop hook, fabric-config.json invisible to users, /fabric-import recommendation missed on first SessionStart) plus the upstream MCP SDK misuse bug they exposed (fab_review tool fully broken because z.discriminatedUnion is incompatible with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.29.0 registerTool). Four fully parallel tasks, zero file overlap, with a single end-of-chain Gemini review + coverage pass.
approach: Four independent tasks executed in parallel: (1) TASK-001 splits fab_review's input/output into a flat ZodRawShape for SDK registration while keeping the discriminatedUnion as the internal authoritative contract. (2) TASK-002 adds an isImportInFlight gate to fabric-hint.cjs Signal B (silencing only B during active imports) and dismantles the sentinel mechanism on the hook side. (3) TASK-003 makes init.ts scaffold a discoverable default fabric-config.json with every reader-consumed field and removes the maybeWriteImportSentinel path + its clack confirm. (4) TASK-004 replaces the retired sentinel with a deterministic SessionStart underseed self-check in knowledge-hint-broad.cjs (with a revision_hash-bypass for the banner) and cleans the dead sentinel sections from the fabric-import SKILL.md. Each task touches a disjoint file set, so all four can run in any order or fully in parallel; sentinel-removal is coordinated via a shared retirement design (each task owns its own surface area).
estimated_time: 3-4 hours (4 parallel tasks, ~45-60 min each, plus end-of-chain review/coverage)

design_decisions.decision: Two-layer schema for fab_review (flat ZodRawShape for SDK + discriminatedUnion as internal authoritative contract)
design_decisions.rationale: MCP SDK 1.29.0 validateToolOutput requires z.object()-shape input/output; passing a discriminatedUnion crashes on schema._zod access. Splitting the surface preserves discoverable JSON Schema (ToolSearch sees all fields) and runtime cross-field strictness simultaneously, with no client-visible API change.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Two parallel definitions must stay in sync (mitigated by collocated definitions and a drift-guard unit test).
alternatives: Wrap in z.object({ payload: union }) (breaks all clients)

design_decisions.decision: Reuse .fabric/.import-state.json (already written by fabric-import skill checkpoints) as the in-flight signal for fabric-hint.cjs Signal B gating
design_decisions.rationale: Zero new state files, zero new contract; piggybacks on an artifact the import skill already maintains. Keeps the never-block invariant by treating any read/parse error as 'not in flight'.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Tightly couples hook behaviour to an internal skill artifact; mitigated by treating the file's existence as a soft signal only.

design_decisions.decision: Hard-code IMPORT_IN_FLIGHT_MAX_AGE_HOURS = 24 instead of reading from fabric-config.json
design_decisions.rationale: YAGNI per design lock; 24h is a conservative upper bound for any real import session. Adding a config knob would expand the discoverability surface without a real user need.
design_decisions.tradeoff: If a user truly needs a longer window they must edit hook source; acceptable for an edge case.
alternatives: Use much shorter TTL like 1h (too aggressive for a slow import)

design_decisions.decision: Retire the .fabric/.import-requested sentinel mechanism entirely (clean-slate per zero-user-period preference) rather than patching its non-interactive write paths
design_decisions.rationale: The sentinel's failure modes are systemic: every non-interactive init path (CI, -y, piped input, --plan, FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE=1, TTY-detection failures) bypasses the clack confirm and never writes the file, leading to silent SessionStart. A SessionStart self-check on agents.meta.json + canonical count is deterministic and removes the entire failure surface.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Banner re-emits on every SessionStart until import completes or knowledge graph crosses the threshold; acceptable for a one-line hint.
alternatives: Keep sentinel and force-write in non-interactive paths (still racy across many entry points)
Lower Stop-hook Signal C TTL (still misses same-day first session)

design_decisions.decision: Each task owns a disjoint file set; sentinel removal is split across TASK-002 (fabric-hint.cjs), TASK-003 (init.ts), TASK-004 (knowledge-hint-broad.cjs + SKILL.md) so all four tasks can execute fully in parallel without merge conflicts
design_decisions.rationale: Zero file overlap means parallel execution is safe; design coherence is preserved because all three tasks remove their own surface area of the same retired mechanism.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Must verify no cross-file import of removed sentinel identifiers (each task does its own grep).
alternatives: Single 'remove sentinel' task spanning multiple files (serialises execution unnecessarily)

design_decisions.decision: Banner in knowledge-hint-broad.cjs bypasses the existing revision_hash gate (per-line emit decision)
design_decisions.rationale: If the knowledge graph hash hasn't changed, the broad-summary body is correctly suppressed — but the import recommendation must still surface or it would be invisible until the user does something to change the graph (chicken-and-egg).
design_decisions.tradeoff: Adds a small special-case to the emit logic; verified by a unit test asserting broad-summary body is NOT re-emitted on unchanged hash even when the banner IS emitted.
alternatives: Always emit broad summary too (over-emits hint noise)
Reset revision_hash on init (fragile and out-of-band)

design_decisions.decision: Run Gemini review + coverage ONCE at end of the 4-task chain, not per-task
design_decisions.rationale: Per project memory `feedback_review_batching.md` — for multi-task lite-plan chains the user prefers a single end-of-chain review pass to reduce review fatigue and surface cross-task interactions.
design_decisions.tradeoff: If a task has a defect it propagates further before review catches it; mitigated by each task having strong per-task convergence criteria + unit tests as first-line gates.

conventions: Hooks live in packages/cli/templates/hooks/ as standalone .cjs files (no cross-hook require)
Never-block invariant: hooks must never produce decision:'block' due to internal errors
Idempotent file writes for init artifacts (test-then-write, no overwrite)
shared_context.design_lock: All four tasks carry pre-grilled, locked design decisions. Implementing agents must NOT re-debate trade-offs; they may flag implementation-level issues (e.g. 'last_checkpoint_at field name doesn't match assumption') and adapt mechanically.

dimensions.completeness: All 4 spec'd requirements (1 bug + 3 UX) mapped to tasks; all referenced files included as targets
dimensions.granularity: Each task ~45-60 min scope; no task spans more than 4 source files + their tests
dimensions.dependencies: depends_on=[] for all 4 tasks; zero file overlap verified across files[].path; no circular deps possible
dimensions.convergence_criteria: All criteria are quantified/testable (file existence checks, grep-zero invariants, JSON.parse success, exact field counts, named test cases)
dimensions.implementation_steps: Each task has 6-9 numbered implementation steps with explicit ordering
dimensions.constraint_compliance: Follows zero-user clean-slate preference (sentinel retirement), end-of-chain review preference, never-block invariant, rc.8 naming continuity
